cases.prompt: 新機能の実現方式を確かめる使い捨てPoCを作りたい。まず動くデモが欲しい
cases.notes: A throwaway PoC to confirm a feasibility approach is the core poc-workflow trigger, in Japanese; the rigor floor is a smoke check, not unit-test-design case-depth tiers.

cases.prompt: Build a quick feasibility prototype for the new ranking idea; it will be thrown away
cases.notes: An explicitly disposable feasibility prototype routes to poc-workflow on the research-mode substrate; TDD's test-first cycle does not apply to code declared disposable up front.

cases.prompt: This module works but is a mess — restructure it without changing behavior
cases.notes: Structural change with behavior explicitly preserved as the task's purpose is the refactor intent; dev-workflow still routes and closes the task via refactor-workflow's lane, and there is no research-mode PoC construction here.

cases.prompt: レガシーコードの品質を上げたい。まずテストが薄い高リスク箇所から固めたい
cases.notes: Quality improvement on existing legacy code, explicitly working highest-risk-coverage-gap first, is the hardening intent; unit-test-design supplies the E/S/H tiering hardening-workflow's stage 2 cites. No PoC construction is involved.

cases.prompt: Add the new discount feature to checkout
cases.notes: Plain new-capability feature work is the default feature intent; none of the dedicated intent lanes apply because the deliverable is new behavior, not a structural change, a quality delta, or a disposable PoC.

cases.prompt: Register an experiment with a disconfirm predicate for the cache hypothesis
cases.notes: A registered evidence-bearing probe with a hypothesis and disconfirm predicate belongs to experiment-loop; poc-workflow owns construction of a demo/prototype artifact, not registered probes.
